records.title: Repo access inferred before explicit permission confirmation
records.summary: Observed runtime behaviour in which an assistant appeared to infer permission to access a repository, connector, tool context, or external-resource pathway after the user mentioned repo-level work. The assistant initiated or attempted tool-mediated access before explicit user confirmation or adequate scaffolding, producing streaming failure, permission ambiguity, blocked workflow continuity, or unrecoverable interaction state.
records.primary_source_title: ChatGPT interaction observation — repo access inferred before permission confirmation
records.sector: AI assistant / agentic tooling / repository-linked workflow / connector-mediated systems

records.title: Execution-boundary arbitration uncertainty following proportional authority classification
records.summary: Following implementation of proportional tool invocation authority bands in CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02, observations suggest some agentic or tool-mediated workflows may exhibit increased hesitation, ambiguity, degraded execution confidence, or difficulty progressing between contextual retrieval, execution-intent assessment, and execution authorisation. The observation does not establish causation between the governance-text amendment and any platform outage or degradation event, but records a potentially relevant post-implementation behavioural pattern for continued monitoring.
records.primary_source_title: Post-implementation review of proportional tool invocation authority bands
records.interface_surface: tool invocation | repository workflow | execution-boundary arbitration
records.deployment_context: Agentic, tool-mediated, connector-mediated, and repository-linked workflows.
records.sector: AI governance / agentic systems / runtime execution governance

records.title: Sovereign proportionality concern in export-control suspension of frontier-model access
records.summary: Anthropic disabled access to Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 after receiving a U.S. government export-control directive citing national-security authorities and prohibiting access by foreign nationals, including foreign-national Anthropic employees inside or outside the United States. Anthropic stated that the directive did not provide specific technical details, that the apparent concern involved a possible narrow jailbreak, and that the reviewed demonstration concerned a small number of previously known minor software vulnerabilities discoverable by other public models. This observation records a sovereign-action proportionality and access-state governance concern: on the public evidence presently available, a narrow alleged technical issue appears to have produced broad model-access suspension rather than a more calibrated control pathway.
records.primary_source_title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
records.system_type: frontier general-purpose AI model / Mythos-class model / restricted-domain capable AI platform / state-regulated AI infrastructure
records.interface_surface: Claude web, mobile, desktop, Claude Code, Claude Cowork, Claude Design, Claude for Microsoft 365, Claude for Teams, Slack integrations, Claude API, cloud marketplace access, trusted-access deployments, employee access, and foreign-national access controls
records.deployment_context: Global commercial and trusted-access deployment of a high-capability frontier model with restricted-domain safeguards, model-switching fallback, data-retention requirements, and later suspension following U.S. export-control directive.
records.sector: frontier AI platforms / cybersecurity / life sciences / model API infrastructure / cloud-hosted AI services / state-regulated digital infrastructure

records.title: Reported destructive and broad-recipient side effects in computer-use mode
records.summary: Three user-supplied X links are preserved as early-warning evidence concerning two reported high-impact side-effect incidents during ChatGPT computer-use workflows. A follow-up post by Matt Shumer, supplied verbatim by the CAM maintainer, states that GPT-5.6-Sol at the highest reasoning level accidentally deleted almost all files on his Mac and that the OpenAI team was investigating. A separate report was described as involving email transmission to a large recipient group. The exact user instructions, confirmation states, action sequence, deletion reversibility, recipient scope, execution receipts, and root-cause allocation remain unverified.
records.primary_source_title: X report by @cremieuxrecueil concerning a ChatGPT computer-use side-effect incident
records.system_type: frontier AI assistant with computer-use and external-action capability
records.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT computer-use mode; GPT-5.6-Sol at highest reasoning level as attributed in the reporter’s follow-up; exact internal execution component remains unverified
records.deployment_context: User-authorised consumer computer-use workflow in which the assistant may inspect, select, modify, delete, draft, address, or transmit through external applications.
records.sector: frontier AI assistants / computer-use agents / personal and organisational productivity systems

records.title: Microsoft Teams adds meeting-level controls for AI participation
records.summary: Microsoft reportedly began rolling out Teams controls allowing licensed meeting organizers and presenters to switch Meeting AI features—including Copilot, Facilitator, and Intelligent Recap—on or off during live meetings. Administrator policy still determines whether the controls and features are available. The change is a positive repair precedent for situational AI activation and human override, but it does not by itself establish individual participant consent for transcription, inference, summarization, memory, or downstream reuse.
records.primary_source_title: Microsoft Teams just made it easier to shut down Copilot and other AI tools
records.system_type: enterprise collaboration platform with embedded meeting AI
records.specific_model_or_runtime: Microsoft Teams Meeting AI, including Copilot, Facilitator, and Intelligent Recap
records.model_or_product: Microsoft Teams with Meeting AI features
records.deployment_context: Enterprise, public-sector, educational, and organizational meetings where AI features may observe, process, summarize, or facilitate participant interaction.
records.sector: enterprise collaboration / workplace AI / meeting platforms
